summary:
The query asks which muscle does NOT insert into the greater tubercle of the humerus; according to standard anatomical knowledge, while supraspinatus, infraspinatus, and teres minor all attach to it, subscapularis inserts onto the lesser tubercle instead. Thus, subscapularis is the correct answer as per fundamental anatomical teachings.

This summary reflects core clinical understanding essential for accurate diagnosis and intervention planning related to shoulder pathology where precise muscular attachments are critical considerations during physical examination or procedural guidance.
